mnemonics: C.O.S.T. (Criteria for Success): Customer satisfaction, On-time delivery, Staying within budget, Technical objectives met
keyPoints: Success is multi-dimensional, often defined by meeting stakeholder expectations, not just technical specs.
Failure is frequently due to poor requirements gathering, changing scope, and inadequate communication.
The Standish Group's CHAOS Report identifies key success factors like user involvement, executive support, and clear requirements.
commonMistakes: Focusing solely on the 'Iron Triangle' (Time, Cost, Scope) while ignoring product quality and stakeholder satisfaction.
Not managing changing requirements through a formal process, leading to scope creep.
Assuming a project is a failure if it's late or over budget, without considering the delivered business value.
